subject: ap-chemistry
title: Intermolecular Forces & Properties
stimulus:
3. A student isgiven the task of determining the molar concentration of a CuSO solution using two different
procedures, precipitation and spectrophotometry.
For the precipitation experiment, the student adds 20.0 mL of 0.200 M Ba(NO ) to 50.0 mL of the CuSO (aq).
3 2 4
The reaction goes to completion, and a white precipitate forms. The student filters the precipitate and dries it
overnight. The data are given in the following table.
Mass of dry filter paper 0.764 g
Volume of CuSO (aq) 50.0 mL
Volume of 0.200 M Ba( NO ) 20.0 mL
Mass of filter paper and dried precipitate 1.136 g

parts.prompt: Write a balanced net ionic equation for the precipitation reaction.
rubric_criteria: For the correct balanced equation (state symbols not required): 1 point

parts.prompt: Calculate the number of moles of precipitate formed.
rubric_criteria: For the correct calculated value of the mass of precipitate (may be implicit): 1 point
For the correct calculated value of the number of moles, consistent with mass of precipitate: 1 point

parts.prompt:
Calculate the molarity of the original CuSO solution.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

For the spectrophotometry experiment, the student first makes a standard curve. The student uses a 0.1000 M
solution of CuSO (aq ) to make three more solutions of known concentration (0 .0500 M , 0.0300 M , and
0.0100 M ) in 50.00 mL volumetric flasks.
rubric_criteria: For the correct calculated value, consistent with part (b): 1 point

parts.prompt:
Calculate the volume of 0.1000 M CuSO (aq ) needed to make 50.00 mL of 0.0500 M CuSO (aq ).
4 4
rubric_criteria: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

parts.prompt:
Briefly describe the procedure the student should follow to make 50.00 mL of 0.0500 M CuSO (aq ) using
0.1000 M CuSO (aq ),a 50.00 mL volumetric flask, and other standard laboratory equipment. Assume that all
appropriate safety precautions will be taken.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

The standard curve isgiven below.
rubric_criteria: For a correct technique to measure the volume of solution: 1 point
First, measure out the correct volume of 0.1000 M CuSO solution with a 25.0 mL
For a correct technique to dilute the solution to the final volume: 1 point
Transfer the 25.0 mL of solution to a 50.00 mL volumetric flask and dilute the solution with

parts.prompt:
The absorbance of the CuSO solution of unknown concentration is 0.219. Determine the molarity of the
solution.
rubric_criteria: For the correct value (between 0.032 M and 0.038 M): 1 point

parts.prompt:
A second student performs the same experiment. There are a few drops of water in the cuvette before the
second student adds the CuSO (aq ) so lution of unknown concentration. Will this result ina CuSO (aq )
concentration for the unknown that is greater than, less than, or equal to the concentration determined in
part (f) ? Justify your answer.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.
rubric_criteria: The concentration will be less than that determined in part (f).
